The classification of Chrysothemis semiclausa also places it within the order Lamiales, which includes a wide range of flowering plant families.

intro: Chrysothemis semiclausa, commonly known as the 'Blue Hairy Fiddlehead', is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Chrysothemis semiclausa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
